headlines.title: Parents of Southport survivors say anonymity has erased their girls from the story
headlines.description: The BBC has spoken to parents of some of the children who survived the knife attack, who believe they have been given insufficient support because of the anonymity order.

headlines.title: Andrew investigation could look into sexual misconduct allegations
headlines.description: The former prince was arrested in February on suspicion of misconduct in public office.

headlines.title: Stop blaming young people for being unemployed, says Amazon's UK boss
headlines.description: John Boumphrey says the education system "isn't necessarily producing young people who are ready for work".

headlines.title: Guardiola to leave Man City after 10 years as boss
headlines.description: Manchester City confirm that Pep Guardiola will leave at the end of the season after a decade of unparalleled success.

headlines.title: Riskiest skin cancer cases hit UK record high
headlines.description: The number of melanoma skin cancer cases has risen above 20,000 a year for the first time in the UK.

headlines.title: Rubio says Cuba is threat to US as Havana accuses him of 'lies'
headlines.description: Cuba's foreign minister accused Rubio of trying to "instigate a military aggression".

headlines.title: Reality TV risks sinking 'into the gutter', warns ex-Channel 4 and Ofcom boss Michael Grade
headlines.description: Michael Grade's warning comes in the wake of allegations made by participants on Married at First Sight.

headlines.title: Rape sentences for teen boys unduly lenient, says Jess Phillips
headlines.description: The three teenagers avoided custodial sentences for their roles in the rape of two girls.

headlines.title: Morrisons planning to close 100 stores in next few months
headlines.description: It said difficulties had been exacerbated by "significant cost increases resulting from government policy choices".

headlines.title: Alberta to hold referendum on whether to remain in Canada
headlines.description: Voters in the western province will decide next October whether they want to stay in Canada or move ahead with a process for a binding independence referendum.

headlines.title: Maisie Peters on change, having a thick skin and her 'healed' sound
headlines.description: The singer shares what it has been like to grow up with her fans and the inspiration behind her new album.

headlines.title: Cubans grapple with fuel shortages and blackouts as US steps up pressure
headlines.description: The BBC speaks to Havana residents after the US charges the country's former leader, Raúl Castro, with murder.

headlines.title: 'Monitoring the situation': Why young men are drawn to prediction markets
headlines.description: Prediction markets are a fast-growing, multi-billion-dollar industry, and appeal disproportionately to young men

headlines.title: 'We're meant to dance': Radio 1's Big Weekend has one focus on its first day
headlines.description: Thousands are expected to attend this year's festival in Sunderland, with Fatboy Slim headlining on Friday.

headlines.title: Hamnet author Maggie O'Farrell on her new novel and keeping her Bafta in the basement
headlines.description: Maggie O'Farrell's new novel, Land, is published after the success of the film adaptation of Hamnet.

headlines.title: 'We are resilient': As San Diego's Muslim community reels from mosque shooting, it refuses to be intimidated
headlines.description: Thousands attend funeral prayers for the three victims of Monday's deadly shooting at the Islamic Centre of San Diego.

headlines.title: Weekly quiz: Eurovision went mad for Bangaranga - but who gave the UK its only point?
headlines.description: How much attention did you pay to what happened in the world over the past seven days?

headlines.title: Jessie J 'sobbed for hours' after hearing she is cancer-free
headlines.description: The singer says she "exhaled for the first time in a year" after doctors gave her the news.

headlines.title: Stephen Colbert joined by Sir Paul McCartney for The Late Show finale
headlines.description: The host joined Sir Paul and the band to close out the show with a performance of the Beatles' Hello, Goodbye.

headlines.title: Nato chief welcomes US sending 5,000 troops to Poland
headlines.description: President Trump's announcement on Thursday night came ahead of a meeting of Nato foreign ministers.

headlines.title: Queues at Dover start to build in first holiday getaway since new EU checks
headlines.description: It is the first holiday period since the EU's new border system was fully implemented.

headlines.title: Former Green Party by-election candidate apologises for sharing 'false flag' post
headlines.description: The Green Party had announced Chris Kennedy withdrew as candidate for "personal and family reasons".

headlines.title: Borrowing in April hit highest level since Covid
headlines.description: Borrowing was higher than expected last month and retail sales fell as fuel prices surged.

headlines.title: Slow Food founder Carlo Petrini dies aged 76
headlines.description: Petrini's movement rejected fast food to promote sustainable production and traditional cooking.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: The Rachel Reeves "Fun" Tax Cuts
headlines.description: Cheaper amusement parks, theatre tickets and kid's meals.

headlines.title: Trump gets 'forever' immunity on his past tax affairs
headlines.description: The US president's extraordinary new deal for himself and members of his family

headlines.title: Guardiola says 'nothing is eternal' as Man City exit confirmed
headlines.description: Manchester City confirm that Pep Guardiola will leave at the end of the season after a decade of unparalleled success.

headlines.title: Carrick confirmed as Man Utd permanent boss
headlines.description: Manchester United confirm the appointment of Michael Carrick as their permanent head coach on a two-year contract.

headlines.title: Foden, Palmer, Alexander-Arnold to miss World Cup
headlines.description: Phil Foden, Cole Palmer and Trent Alexander-Arnold have not been included in Thomas Tuchel's 26-man England squad for this summer's World Cup.

headlines.title: How a kickboxing legend & Statham's pal landed a shot at Usyk
headlines.description: Dutchman Rico Verhoeven discusses his kickboxing career, friendship with A-list celebrities and how he landed a shot at Oleksandr Usyk.

headlines.title: Palmer and Foden lose ruthless Tuchel's battle for number 10 roles
headlines.description: Two years ago Phil Foden and Cole Palmer were England's future. Now, they cannot even get into the squad amid fierce rivalry for the number 10 role.
